TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr-fishing_Muu

S.FORM: sua iku ia Ka’angiana Mu’u sua nganar aku.
S.TRANSL: 我是Ka’angiana Mu’u，(那是)是我的名字。
I am Ka’angiana Mu’u, that is my name.

S.FORM: pʉʉ te ku pʉ’ʉna sua makai pinarupu misee.
S.TRANSL: 我要講比如說像釣魚的故事。
I’m going to tell a story about fishing.

S.FORM: sua pinarupu misee ia cucuru sua ka’an saihu ia ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ pinarupu sii sua pinarupu ia tikirim pi kita piacapa.
S.TRANSL: 如果說釣魚，不是很懂的話就不會釣魚, 因為釣魚我們要會懂先找釣竿。
Speaking of fishing, if we go fishing, you don’t know how to fish, then we find the fishing pole first.

S.FORM: sua piacapa kisʉʉn ia noo ka kia pu’a namanamarʉ sua piacapa ia te kita kamanʉng takacicin kapani.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們沒有在平地買的話，釣竿我們要從桂竹那裏自己做。
If we cannot buy the fishing pole on the plain (in the city), we will make it on our own.

S.FORM: sua kapani ia mʉngʉtʉn pi kita kapani.
S.TRANSL: 我們要先砍桂竹。
We need to cut down bamboo sticks.

S.FORM: nakai ’inmamia ’apianarasʉ kitana noo te ci kita pinarupu ia kumakoru pi kita ngica’ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 只是當我們要釣魚的時候，只是讓我們困擾的是, 我們還要挖蚯蚓。
If we’re to go fishing, we need to dig hard for earthworms.

S.FORM: kumakoru pi kita ngʉca’ʉ tia pʉn.
S.TRANSL: 要先挖蚯蚓做餌。
We dig for earthworms and get them on the hook.

S.FORM: sua pʉn sua pʉn ia noo kai pakinen na narupu ia ka’an tia tavarʉ’ʉʉn marinarupu sua vutukuru.
S.TRANSL: 鉤呢，如果沒有附著在魚鉤上，(不然)就不會鉤到那個魚。
We hook them up, (otherwise) we won’t be able to catch the fish.

S.FORM: sii sua vutukuru ia ’inmamia ngʉca’ʉ sua kʉn en.
S.TRANSL: 因為魚呢，只有吃蚯蚓。
Because the fish only eat earthworms.

S.FORM: mata te pi kia pu’a narupu pu’a ’uracʉ kumiri na sua tai Uh.
S.TRANSL: 另外我們還要買魚鉤, 買魚絲, 栓在...

S.FORM: piacapa.
S.TRANSL: 釣竿。
Fishing pole.

S.FORM: kiriin ci nia sua ’uracʉ.
S.TRANSL: 把魚線栓在那。

S.FORM: kumiri nia sua narupu.
S.TRANSL: 把鉤子栓在那邊。
The fishing thread is tied up there on the hook.

S.FORM: ka’an ’inmamia narupu sua kiria ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 不是只有魚鉤要栓在那邊。
It’s not just about the hook where it (the thread) is tied.

S.FORM: kirin pa nia ’uracʉ kumiri inia narupu ʉsʉ’ʉʉn cu.
S.TRANSL: 先要把魚絲栓在那哩，再把鉤子栓在那裡，然後再放鉛。
We still have to tie the thread first, and then tie it on the hook.

S.FORM: noo kai isee varʉvarʉ ia ka’an kita tavarʉ’ʉ pinarupu.
S.TRANSL: 如果沒有放鉛的話，我們就不能釣魚。
If we don’t put VARUVARU, then we cannot fish.

S.FORM: sua pʉn kisʉʉn ia ngica’ʉ.
S.TRANSL: 所謂的魚餌就是蚯蚓。
The bait here means the earthworm.

S.FORM: noo kakia saihu tekirim tia pʉn ia ka’an kita tavarʉ’ʉ pinarupu.
S.TRANSL: 如果不會找要當作魚餌的，我們就沒辦法釣魚。
If we’re not able to find any to serve as the bait, we cannot go fishing.

S.FORM: sua pinarupu misee ia mokusa kia na ’aci.
S.TRANSL: 講到釣魚，我們去河流的交匯處。
To fish, we go to the converging point of the rivers.

S.FORM: sua ’aci kisʉʉn ia kan tavarʉ’ʉ kam.
S.TRANSL: 這個所謂的'aci(河流匯集處)你們知道嗎 !?
You should know about the so-called ‘aaci’ (converging point of rivers).

S.FORM: necʉre sua ’aci kisʉʉn ia caniava noo pomiri’in sua cakʉran.
S.TRANSL: 如果河水滿起來了…
If the river level rises…

S.FORM: ’una ci tavʉna niaran rʉmʉra nonnon tingee kisʉʉn.
S.TRANSL: 從山上流下來的溪水就是小溪。
There’s a stream flowing from the mountain, and it’s called ‘nonnon’.

S.FORM: ’una ’aci sumakusa cakʉran na cikura ia pitakusʉʉn cu nia narupu.
S.TRANSL: 有河流匯集處流著(因颱風造成的)髒水，魚鉤就丟到那了。
There’s the converging point where the muddied water flows, and the hook is tossed there.

S.FORM: noo pinarupʉʉn ci ia ruma’esi ci nia vutukuru ia arisʉ’ʉrʉʉn cu mita makai vutukuru mitatatiting cu nia vutukuru makasia tatia na.
S.TRANSL: 在釣魚時，魚咬那個鉤子的話，那邊搖來搖去，魚很大。
When fishing, if the fish bites the hook, it’ll shake there, and the fish is that big.

S.FORM: ka’an tavarʉ’ʉ po’isua sua.
S.TRANSL: 不可以說關於...
We cannot talk about ...

S.FORM: necʉre sua pinarupu misee ia.
S.TRANSL: 你看，說到釣魚(這件事)。
You see, speaking of the fishing.

S.FORM: noo kaa kia nipu’a punapunarʉ sua piacapa ia mosikarʉ pi kia rakakapania.
S.TRANSL: 如果我們沒有在平地買到釣竿的話，就要進到有很多竹子的地方。
If we cannot buy the fishing pole on the plain, we will need to enter areas where there are bamboos.

S.FORM: mariparici avarai noo akia ci ia kan mongʉtʉn ci kia mamia to’omang noo akia ci kapani.
S.TRANSL: 如果沒有Kapani這個竹子的話，就只好砍(任何其他的)粗的竹子。
If there is not Kapani bamboo, we can only cut down any other bamboos.

S.FORM: apacoee noo akia ci kapani ia mongʉtʉn ci kia to’omang ma’ura.
S.TRANSL: 雖然如果沒有那個小竹子，(我們)就再砍較小一點的粗竹子。
If there isn’t the bamboo, we will cut down other smaller bamboos.

S.FORM: noo ka kia mʉtʉa to’omang ia mongʉtʉn kia ma kanavunavu.
S.TRANSL: 我們如果沒有找到(適合)的竹子，我(們)就(隨便)砍Kanavunavu這種竹子。
If we don’t find the right bamboo, we simply cut down the Kanavunavu bamboo.

S.FORM: kumiri ’inia sua narupu pitatavʉrʉ cacanumu.
S.TRANSL: 把釣鉤綁在竹子上，丟到水裡。
(We) tie up the hook onto the bamboo, and toss it into the water.

S.FORM: ruma’esi ci ’inia vutukuru ia ’an cu arikʉnʉʉn sii mecun sua kapani mecun sua vutukuru.
S.TRANSL: 魚咬到(上鉤)他的話，拉不動了，因為竹子很重，魚(也)很重。
When the fish bites it, it becomes hard to pull, because the bamboo is heavy and the fish is heavy too.

S.FORM: mʉʉ (noo) nimarinarupu ci kia ia ’esi ci makai sana cau ia.
S.TRANSL: 如果(當)釣到魚之後，人比如在那邊了。
If I have caught the fish, and there are people over there.

S.FORM: marisusunu kia ioo misee kia noo nimarinarupu ci kia sua vutukuru.
S.TRANSL: 當我們吊上魚的時候，我們就會呼喊耶~~。
When we catch the fish, we would yell, ‘ioooo’.

S.FORM: tarakusa cu kitana cau ia’a.
S.TRANSL: 人就望著我們看。
People look back at us.

S.FORM: imo sua ikita po’ʉ’ʉna ia te kita masi manʉng pokarikari manasi.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們講故事的時候，我們要說清楚。
So, when we tell stories, we have to speak clearly.

S.FORM: ka’an kia masimuci.
S.TRANSL: 我們才不會說錯。
Then we won’t say incorrectly.

S.FORM: uh makai uh muru’ucang.
S.TRANSL: 像結婚。
Like getting married.

S.FORM: sua tenannakʉ ia makasi.
S.TRANSL: 要求婚這樣。
Like proposing.

S.FORM: sua kanakanavu miana ia mokusa nia tia ng(u)coo amisen sua kisaa Sumukun.
S.TRANSL: 以前卡那卡那富人要去那裡，布農族話要怎麼說呢 ?
In the past, Kanakanavu people would go there

S.FORM: uh tia ponannakʉ misee.
S.TRANSL: 就是要去‘求婚’。
It’s the so-called ‘propose’.

S.FORM: sua ponannakʉ ia mokusa kia nia ka’an kia masimanʉng cucuru.
S.TRANSL: 求婚的話，我們會去那裏(女方家)，不會很直接地說。
To propose, we would go there, and we wouldn’t say it very clearly.

S.FORM: pokarikari kia ah nivatu kia te kia kan masitanam makarikari kamua.
S.TRANSL: 我們來，試著要跟你們說。
We came to try to talk to you.

S.FORM: uh man tarikukua mu te kara kia e tavarʉ’ʉ aracani tia apacʉvʉrʉʉn misee kia mamia sua kari.
S.TRANSL: 我們只有這樣說這些話 : 我能不能拿一隻你們的小雞來養呢?
We only say these words, ‘Can I take one of your little chicken to breed?’

S.FORM: uh sua kari isi ia kan ’una ’inia tava ma uh mara’an akanang.
S.TRANSL: 這樣的話，那邊有些(人)很快就聽懂。
These words, some people there can understand quickly.

S.FORM: nakai sua makai ikia naturu ia ka’an mara’an akanang sii manasʉ amisen sua cau isi manasʉ misee.
S.TRANSL: 但像我比較笨的，不會很快聽得懂，因為也許是(懷疑)這個人為什麼(這樣)講。
But for someone as stupid as I am, we cannot understand quickly, because we don’t know why this person say things like this.

S.FORM: nokai manasʉ te pa kesone.
S.TRANSL: 或可能還要再詢問。
Or maybe we still need to ask.

S.FORM: aah ’una kara kasu man(u) nanakʉ nate ku ponannakʉ.
S.TRANSL: 你有沒有女孩子，我想要求婚。
Do you have any girl that I wanted?

S.FORM: te maku arʉʉn m misee kia.
S.TRANSL: 我就說，我要娶回來。
I say, ‘I want to marry (her)’.

S.FORM: aah makasua nava kari in isia va.
S.TRANSL: 他的話原來是那樣。
His words are like that.

S.FORM: ponannakʉ kia misee.
S.TRANSL: 原來對方是在跟我們求婚這樣。
(Now I see) he is proposing to us.

S.FORM: uh makasua sua ponannakʉ kisʉʉn.
S.TRANSL: 所謂的求婚就是那樣!
The so-called proposing is like that.

S.FORM: nakai miana ikita Kanakanavu ia apacoe noo marivari in ia te kara kia miapacai nenen kusa tarikuka kara sua tutui kara?.
S.TRANSL: 但是以前我們卡族呢，雖然若對方已經答覆了，我們是不是要宰殺什麼呢?究竟是雞還是豬?
But we Kanakanavu people, in the past, if they responded, shall we kill any animals? Should it be chicken or pigs?

S.FORM: ka’an pi kia tavarʉ’ʉ nakai noo pacʉpcʉpʉng ku ia ikita kanakanavu mamarang miana ia makai tia ucan mamia tarikuka serupaca noo.
S.TRANSL: 我們還不知道，但是我若(仔細地)想的話，以前卡族的老人家好像只要用一隻雞。
We don’t know, but when I think about it, Kanakanavu people used only one chicken in the past.

S.FORM: caniava noo marivari in sua tamna manu su man nanakʉ.
S.TRANSL: 假如擁有女孩子的(長輩)答應的話。
If the parents of the girl said yes.

S.FORM: nakai te kara te kara kia pu’a paici ’apima ’inia ka’an kara?.
S.TRANSL: 但是我(們)是不是要買酒給他們喝，還是不用?
But, do we have to but them some wine to drink or not ?

S.FORM: uh makasua sua.
S.TRANSL: 就是那樣。
It’s just like that.

S.FORM: uh kanakanavu miana tie en ng(u)coo mosa cocua ponannakʉ ’una kara kasu ka’an masimanʉng mara’an sua.
S.TRANSL: 以前卡族人若要去別家求婚的話，你有沒有很快的說清楚。
When Kanakanavu people went to other families to propose, would you say clearly and quickly.

S.FORM: kanakanavu miana sua ’una kasu manu nanakʉ misee.
S.TRANSL: 以前的卡族人就說你有沒有女孩子。
Kanakanavu people used to say, ‘Do you have girls?’

S.FORM: una kara kasu man tarikuka m te m nate kia masitanam nate kia tanam ’apacʉvʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 你有沒有小雞呢，我原本想說要試著飼養。
Do you have any little chicken? I wanted to try to breed.

S.FORM: misee misee kia mamia.
S.TRANSL: 我(們)只(用這種方式)表達(求婚)。
We only say this (to propose).

S.FORM: sua mamarang iikita mia marang ta miana kanakanavu ia makasua noo noo pokarikari.
S.TRANSL: 我們老人家，我們以前的老人家，講話的時候會那樣。
We the elderly… we the elderly in the past, are like that when speaking.

S.FORM: uh amisen ci misoni natia pocuvunga nia.
S.TRANSL: 阿，剛才原本要說了什麼要接下去?
Uh, what was I going to say just now to continue from there?

S.FORM: nakai te ku pokarikari sua makai cʉpʉng kanakanavu sua kaniarumi.
S.TRANSL: 但是我要說，像卡族人(怎麼看待)對穿山甲的想法。
But I want to say, something like how Kanakanavu people think about the pangolin.

S.FORM: sua ikim kanakanavu kisʉʉn ia vanai ’inia puesia umokʉrʉ mepacai kanirume ia.
S.TRANSL: 我們被稱為卡那卡那富的人，為什麼(因這則故事)忌諱觸摸和宰殺(穿山甲)?
We the so-called Kanakanavu people, why is it a taboo to touch (pangolins)?

S.FORM: ikim kanakanavu ia cucuru uh nipapacʉvʉrʉ kimia sua kanirume.
S.TRANSL: (因為)穿山甲養真的曾經為養過我們卡族人。
(Because) pangolins really had fostered us Kanakanavu people.

S.FORM: nen vanai in?.
S.TRANSL: (他)是什麼原因 ?
Why is it?

S.FORM: sua kana ’una kanakanavu miania uh ’usu kan ngane en.
S.TRANSL: 以前有位卡族人，傳說她名字叫做’Usu。
There was a Kanakanavu person, and it is said that her name was ’Usu.

S.FORM: sua ’usu ia niaraka cu.
S.TRANSL: ’Usu去世了。
’Usu was deceased.

S.FORM: niaraka cu mamane.
S.TRANSL: 他在人間已經去世了。
He was deceased in the world.

S.FORM: maricuvucuvung cau nerʉra kamanʉng maricʉkʉcʉkʉ sua nerʉra tia tasʉa sua niaraka ’usu kisʉʉn.
S.TRANSL: 人們聚集在一起把木板釘起來要放置那位說是已去世的’Usu。
People gathered together and boarded up to place the deceased ’Usu as said.

S.FORM: a’unee cu umavung.
S.TRANSL: 然後背去埋葬。
Then carried to bury.

S.FORM: patirupange cu maricʉkʉcʉkʉ tacucu umavung.
S.TRANSL: 完成插入了木板，送去埋葬。
When (they) finished boarding up, (they) sent it to bury.

S.FORM: sua ’usu ihua niavungan cu misee nen kʉcʉ karianome misen tia tavara’ʉ ’esi ’inia sua ’usu niavunga misee?.
S.TRANSL: 那位’Usu被埋葬了後，究竟穿山甲為什麼會知道’Usu被埋葬在那邊?
After that ’Usu was buried, how on earth could the pangolin know that ’Usu was buried there?

S.FORM: mokusa kan nia karinome takusʉrʉ kan mamia maki makivun makivun makivun makasi.
S.TRANSL: 據說穿山甲過去那裡，一直繞圈，一直撥撥撥這樣。
It is said that the pangolin went there, kept circling around and digging around.

S.FORM: pakitʉn in kan nia sua si’inʉve en.
S.TRANSL: 據說被牠(穿山甲)找到他(’Usu)的門。
It is said that it found his door there.

S.FORM: paritakaree ’inia sua si’inʉve eni.
S.TRANSL: 牠開著他的門。
It opened his door.

S.FORM: aratakarʉ mocan ah ah paci uh patisa’e kan nia s karinume.
S.TRANSL: 他站起來走，抓著穿山甲。
He stood up and walked, and he grabbed it (the pangolin).

S.FORM: sosomanpe kan sosomanpe cu kasu.
S.TRANSL: (說)‘謝謝你了。’
(He said) ‘thanks, thank you.’

S.FORM: iva kasua sua maritakarʉ te pa po’one ’ikua mamane.
S.TRANSL: ‘原來是你抬開讓我還在人間(讓我活著)。’
‘Now I see it’s you who lifted it up and kept me alive.’

S.FORM: aranai ci ’inia sua Kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 卡族人從此就這樣…
Since then, Kanakanavu people…

S.FORM: ’aan mʉkʉrʉ karinume?.
S.TRANSL: 不會碰穿山甲?
Not touch the pangolins?

S.FORM: cisii makasua.
S.TRANSL: 是為了那個原因。
It’s like that.

S.FORM: necire iiku miana sira’ʉ’ee.
S.TRANSL: 你看我以前多強!
Look, I used to be so good!

S.FORM: nimoringe moha kia tantannau nimoringe kim.
S.TRANSL: 我去叢林設陷阱，我們設了陷阱。
I went to the woods to set up traps, and we caught so many pangolins.

S.FORM: sua rʉ’ʉvu mesua ia acu niaraka.
S.TRANSL: 我當時的夥伴已經去世了。
My partner at that time was already deceased.

S.FORM: ap na ’apio ngane en.
S.TRANSL: 他的名字叫’Apio。
His name is ’Apio.

S.FORM: nimoringee kimi.
S.TRANSL: 我們設陷阱。
We set up traps.

S.FORM: sirʉpa’e niporinge mia karianome.
S.TRANSL: 被我們套中的穿山甲不知道有多少。
The number of the pangolins that we caught was so huge.

S.FORM: sii tinmana kia mamarang miana.
S.TRANSL: 因為我曾聽過老人家。
Because I’ve heard (some words of) the elderly people.

S.FORM: pueri’i inia apasavua putukirin cu pariaruma.
S.TRANSL: 吐給牠治療牠，解開(陷阱)然後讓他走。
Spit onto it to cure it, and then unleash the trap and release it.

S.FORM: apaco’e noo aka ci kʉkiri in ia puerin pa nia ’uici.
S.TRANSL: 如果牠的腿受傷了，還要再吐到他的傷口。
If its leg is not good, we still need to spit on that part.

S.FORM: kasasavuana parirumaan cu.
S.TRANSL: 一直給牠治療，然後讓牠離開。
(We) kept curing it, and let it go.

S.FORM: ikim Kanakanavu ikim mamarang Kanakanavu miana ia makasua sua sinituturu ia.
S.TRANSL: 以前我們卡族老人家所說的就是那樣。
What our Kanakanavu elderly people said in the past is like that.

S.FORM: uh makasua cʉpʉng misee.
S.TRANSL: (卡族人的)觀念是那樣。
The (Kanakanavu’s) view is like that.

S.FORM: imo sua ikim Kanakanavu ia ka’an kim pa’apopang.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們卡族人不會隨便。
So we Kanakanavu people won’t do things at will.

S.FORM: uh mataa ’una pa sua uh ucani.
S.TRANSL: 以及還有一個。
And there’s another one.

S.FORM: sua ucani ia uʉ.
S.TRANSL: 一個…
The one uh…

S.FORM: sii sua pusian nokai iname ia kʉʉnʉn tapuroo.
S.TRANSL: 因為稻米如果沒有經過趕鳥，就會被麻雀吃了。
Because the rice, if we don’t expel the birds, it will be eaten by the sparrow.

S.FORM: mokusa nia mocʉmcʉm minana.
S.TRANSL: 趕早去那裏趕鳥。
(He) went there early to expel birds.

S.FORM: makasi cacan mʉtʉa man karianome.
S.TRANSL: 到了路上，無意間發現小隻的穿山甲。
On the way, (he) discovered a little pangolin.

S.FORM: patisaa’e nia pitakusa na serevong ta sikara serevong apacʉvʉrʉ.
S.TRANSL: 他把牠抓起來丟進鐵籠，在鐵籠裡面飼養。
He caught it and put it into the cage, and bred it inside the cage.

S.FORM: matakoru ’uru apakʉn ’inia.
S.TRANSL: 盛飯給牠吃。
Ladle the rice to feed it.

S.FORM: rorovana te cu mosa tanasa.
S.TRANSL: 每晚要去家裡了。
Every night (he) went back home.

S.FORM: avice na tanasa.
S.TRANSL: (他)帶回家。
(He) brought back home.

S.FORM: mʉ nimosa ku nia cʉmʉra mesua.
S.TRANSL: 當時我有去看到。
I went there to have a look at the time.

S.FORM: mocan ku mokusa tanaha tumaturu com coo mia.
S.TRANSL: 我離開回到家裡告訴我們的父親。
I left and went back home to tell our father.

S.FORM: AH cuma te ci mananu nipapacʉvʉrʉ karinome te kara mamanʉng misee.
S.TRANSL: 穿山甲被人家養, 我說這樣好嗎 ?
‘The pangolin is fed (by someone else), is it good?’ I said.

S.FORM: nakai ka’an pakituru.
S.TRANSL: 但他不願意。
But (he) did not want to.

S.FORM: necʉre nen vane Kanakanavu cucuru puesi mihee?.
S.TRANSL: 你看，為什麼說我們卡族人真的忌諱這件事?
You see, why do we Kanakanavu people really consider it a taboo?

S.FORM: aratakarʉ sua Pi’i mesua tia mivu.
S.TRANSL: Pi’i 起來要去小便。
Pi’i stood up then, and went to pee.

S.FORM: makai karinome makasi hua ramuc in.
S.TRANSL: 他的手這樣子像穿山甲。
His hand is like this, like (the skin of) pangolin.

S.FORM: tia mivu.
S.TRANSL: 他要小便。
He wanted to pee.

S.FORM: sua tina’an ini ia kan makai ku kurarung ʉngcoo karinome.
S.TRANSL: 他的身體呢，據說就變得像穿山甲的皮。
His body was said to be like pangolin’s skin.

S.FORM: manasʉ can ucan takananga ka’an.
S.TRANSL: 或許一個小時…不對。
Maybe it’s been one hour…No.

S.FORM: ucan UH ʉngcoo ikuripaee kisʉʉn uh Kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 一個…嗯…就卡語所謂的一個禮拜。
One… one week as it’s called in Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: aranai inia noten mivu ia kan makasi ramuc in makai karianome.
S.TRANSL: 從那時候，若要小便，他的手就這樣子像穿山甲一樣。
Since then, if he wanted to pee, it’s said that his hands would become like the skin of the pangolin.

S.FORM: pʉ pacʉpʉcʉpʉng kim ah cucurua va aka apacʉvʉrʉ imo makasia misee.
S.TRANSL: 我們一直想，的確養穿山甲是不好的事，所以說變這樣。
We are thinking, it’s true that it’s not good to foster pangolins, so it becomes like this.

S.FORM: noten mota’i ia arapipining makasia ramuc in makai karianome.
S.TRANSL: 若要大便，就會出現那樣像穿山甲的手的面貌。
If he wanted to take a shit, his hands would be like the pangolin.

S.FORM: imo ikita Kanakanavu ia akun makasia.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們卡族人不可以這樣子。
So we Kanakanavu people can never do this.

S.FORM: imo ikita Kanakanavu ia ka’an ken sua necire ka’an puesi ia.
S.TRANSL: 所以我們卡族人，你看，不遵守禁忌的...
So, we Kanakanavu people, you see, those who don’t avoid the taboo...

S.FORM: kan poringen kee uh mara karianome.
S.TRANSL: (他)套住然後抓了穿山甲。
(He) caught and took the pangolin.

S.FORM: kamanʉng te maku umora makasi po’ocipi kʉmʉn.
S.TRANSL: 他處理後剁掉煮了吃掉。
He worked on it, chopped and cooked it.

S.FORM: ikita kanakanavu ia ka’an kita tavarʉ’ʉ makasua.
S.TRANSL: 我們卡族人呢，我們絕對不可那樣。
We Kanakanavu people, we cannot do that.

S.FORM: tumanaa ’ikua manasi ka’an kita pamuci.
S.TRANSL: 聽我的話才不會犯錯。
Listen to me, so that we don’t make mistakes.

S.FORM: makasua ma sua setuturu kamua.
S.TRANSL: 我要告訴你們的就是那樣。
What I’m telling you is only that.

S.FORM: uh manman maku arasingsingai.
S.TRANSL: 我的後代晚輩們，要平安喔!
My offspring, may peace be with you !

S.FORM: makasua ku.
S.TRANSL: 我那樣。
Like me.

S.FORM: ʉmʉkʉrʉ pa mamane manasi.
S.TRANSL: 我才能留在世上。
So that I can still live in the world.

S.FORM: makasua kari maku kamua.
S.TRANSL: 我那些給你們的話。
My those words for you.

S.FORM: sosomampe curu.
S.TRANSL: 真的謝謝。
Thanks a lot.

S.FORM: arasingsinga.
S.TRANSL: 祝福平安。
Bless you.
